title: 10 best herbal capsules for mucus in stomach

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with mucus in stomach because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This ingredient helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to excess mucus production. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have mucilaginous properties, meaning it forms a protective coating on the stomach lining that can help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, further reducing mucus buildup and alleviating symptoms of stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation and soothe mucous membranes, allowing for a more effective clearing of excess mucus from the stomach.

Liquiritin: This flavonoid has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm digestive issues such as stomach ulcers and gastritis, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excessive mucus production.
Isomaltol: This compound has been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to thin and loosen thick mucus in the stomach, making it easier to expel.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with mucus in stomach by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of mucus in the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastritis.

Licopyranoside: Contributes to the anti-inflammatory and anti-ulcer properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra, which in turn helps to reduce stomach mucus and alleviate conditions such as gastritis and stomach ulcers.

Isoflavans: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce stomach mucus and alleviate symptoms of IBS and gastritis by protecting the stomach lining and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and thin mucus, making them useful for treating stomach issues.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties and contain glycosides, which can help soothe and protect the stomach lining.
Leaves: The leaves also contain glycosides and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation and mucus in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of glycyrrhiza glabra roots, dried for at least 2 weeks, to ensure optimal quality.
Grind 100g of the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 80g of gelatin, adding 50ml of water and 20ml of glycerin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400mg of the glycyrrhiza glabra mixture, weighing each capsule to ensure accuracy.
Label and store the glycyrrhiza glabra capsules in a cool, dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with mucus in stomach because they contain taraxasterol, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as bloating and cramps. Additionally, dandelion root is known to stimulate digestion and improve liver function, both of which can help to reduce mucus production in the stomach. As a result, taking herbal dandelion capsules may provide relief from excess mucus and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion capsules contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the stomach and gut, helping to break down and remove excess mucus.

Antimicrobial properties: The bitter compounds present in dandelion roots have antimicrobial effects, which can help to eradicate harmful bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to excessive mucus production in the stomach.

Digestive stimulant properties: Dandelion root has been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and improve liver function, which can help to increase digestive enzyme production and reduce the accumulation of mucus in the stomach.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: It helps with mucus in the stomach by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of mucus, thereby alleviating symptoms of digestive issues.
Inulin: It helps by acting as a prebiotic, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria and improving digestion, which in turn reduces mucus production in the stomach.
Friedelin: It helps with mucus in the stomach by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties and reducing the severity of digestive issues such as bloating and gas.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain inulin, a prebiotic that helps regulate digestive health and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: They have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe stomach ulcers and reduce mucus production.
Seeds: They contain a mixture of flavonoids and saponins, which can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves according to standard herbal practice guidelines for maximum potency preservation.
Dry the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves for 2 weeks in a warm dark place at 30-40 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mill for 5 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the taraxacum officinale powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 5 milliliters of water to form a gel-like mixture.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the gel-like mixture using a capsule filler machine and seal them properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with mucus in stomach because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called aloin, which soothes and reduces inflammation in the digestive tract. Additionally, aloe vera has natural mucolytic properties that help break down and dissolve excess mucus, allowing for easier digestion and absorption of nutrients. By reducing mucus buildup, aloe capsules can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, discomfort, and abdominal pain often associated with excessive mucus production in the stomach.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with excess mucus production.

Antioxidant Activity: Herbal aloe capsules contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the stomach lining, preventing damage to the mucous membrane and promoting a healthy gut environment.

Mucilaginous Properties: Aloe vera's mucilage (a thick, protective substance) coats and soothes the stomach lining, protecting it from irritation and inflammation caused by excess mucus production, while also helping to reduce acidity and ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds, particularly aloe-emodin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of mucus-related stomach issues.
Aloin: An anthraquinone glycoside, aloin helps to break down and expel mucus in the stomach, reducing its viscosity and promoting a smoother digestion process.

Glucomannans: These soluble dietary fibers can absorb excess water and swell to form a gel-like substance, which can help to form a barrier against stomach acid and reduce the production of mucus in the stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera due to their high concentration of gel, which contains anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and clear stomach mucus.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce inflammation and clear mucus in the stomach.

Pulp of Leaves: The pulp of the Aloe vera leaves contains a high amount of mucilages, which are gel-like substances that can help to coat and soothe the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and mucus buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera gel from mature aloe vera leaves by cutting off the thick green skin.
Grind the aloe vera gel into a fine powder using a high-speed blender at 10000 rpm.
Mix 500 grams of aloe vera powder with 500 grams of gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1 gram of the aloe vera gelatin mixture using a capsule filler.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 12 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried althaea officinalis root at the end of its growing season for optimal quality.
Grind the root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gum arabic at a ratio of 1:2 for 10 minutes.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the herbal powder mixture for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginkgo biloba extract has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort associated with excess mucus production.

remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and stems dried for 2 hours in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Use a coffee grinder to grind 250 grams of the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder in 5 minutes.
Mix the ginkgo biloba powder with 250 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of glycerin in a large bowl for 10 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill the ginkgo biloba mixture into empty gelatin capsules in 15 minutes.
Label and store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 2 parts of dried st. john's wort roots.

Mix the powder with 2 parts of gelatin in a ratio of 1:2 and add 1 teaspoon of glycerin for 5 minutes.

Mix 250 grams of the powdered rhizome powder with 250 grams of cellulose powder as a filler agent.

remedy_recipe: Dry fresh lavender flowers for at least 24 hours or until they reach a moisture level of 10 percent.
Grind dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix the lavender powder with gelatin and water to form a smooth paste adding one part powder to two parts mixture.
Fill empty capsules with 250 milligrams of the lavender mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.
Store prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with mucus in stomach because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that calms inflammation and soothes the digestive tract. This natural anti-inflammatory reduces swelling and irritation in the gut, allowing mucus to break down more efficiently. Additionally, chamomile's calming properties relax the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramping that can exacerbate mucus buildup. By promoting a healthy balance of digestive enzymes, chamomile capsules support the body's natural detoxification processes, helping to clear excess mucus from the stomach and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compound, apigenin, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the stomach lining, which can alleviate mucus buildup and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by excess mucus, reducing the risk of stomach irritation and discomfort.

Sotheing property: Chamomile's soothing compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have a calming effect on the stomach, helping to reduce spasms, cramps, and digestive discomfort associated with excessive mucus production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Flowers: Contains azulene, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that calms stomach irritation and reduces mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in amounts of 500g for future use.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 200g of ground matricaria chamomilla with 100g of tapioca starch and 50g of gelatin to form a uniform blend.
Fill 250ml-sized capsules with 1 teaspoon each of the herbal blend mixture and seal immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose capsules helps with mucus in stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The capsules' active compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, bind to and neutralize excess mucus, helping to break down and eliminate it from the stomach. This soothing action also reduces inflammation, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, discomfort, and digestive distress, promoting a healthy gut environment and improving overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The Damask Rose capsule's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as stomach discomfort and excess mucus production.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of the Damask Rose capsule help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and mucus production in the process.

Soothing and calming effect: The soothing and calming effects of the Damask Rose capsule can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to excess mucus production and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of vitamin C and antioxidants, which help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and excess mucus production.

Seeds: Damask rose seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate stomach discomfort and reduce inflammation, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules for mucus in stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena flowers for 2 hours early morning.
Dry the flowers using shade drying method for 3 days.
Grind dried rosa damascena flowers into fine powder and mix with 10% silica gel as filler.
Fill gelatin capsules with 200mg of powdered rosa damascena mixture per capsule and seal tightly.
Store finished capsules in airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with mucus in stomach because they contain a compound called flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The capsules also contain a type of acid, known as chamazulene, that has natural antacid and mucilaginous properties, helping to soothe and protect the stomach lining from irritation. As a result, yarrow capsules can effectively dissolve and eliminate excess mucus in the stomach, reducing symptoms such as bloating, gas, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Yarrow capsules have antimicrobial properties that help combat infections and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can reduce excess mucus production.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in yarrow capsules can soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing redness, swelling, and mucus secretion.

Digestive tonic property: Yarrow has been traditionally used as a digestive tonic to stimulate appetite, improve digestion, and increase the flow of digestive enzymes, which can help break down food and reduce mucus buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, making them effective in soothing stomach ulcers and mucus buildup.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which possess expectorant properties that help loosen and clear out excess mucus in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium leaves from your local supplier or online store and measure out 20 grams of material.
Grind the dried achillea millefolium leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground achillea millefolium powder with 50 grams of silica gel capsules in a clean glass bowl to achieve a consistent blend.
Fill empty #0 capsules with the achillea millefolium mixture, ensuring each capsule contains approximately 1 gram of the herbal blend for 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or until expiration date.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for mucus in stomach are natural supplements designed to alleviate excessive mucus production in the stomach, reducing discomfort, bloating, and digestive issues. These capsules are formulated with a blend of effective herbs, including Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Cinnamomum verum, Foeniculum vulgare, Piper nigrum, Trigonella foenum-graecum, Astragalus membranaceus, Cassia angustifolia, and Zanthoxylum bungeanum. If left untreated, stomach mucus can lead to malnutrition, fatigue, and decreased productivity, affecting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to mucus in stomach that are treated with herbal capsules are gastritis, acid reflux, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal capsules containing ingredients like slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Other herbs like turmeric, ginger, and peppermint may also be used to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, gas, and stomach pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with mucus in stomach is a blend of Triphala, Turmeric, and Ginger. Triphala, an Ayurvedic blend of three herbs, helps detoxify the digestive system and reduce inflammation. Turmeric's curcumin reduces inflammation and fights infection, while Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties aid digestion and relieve nausea. This combination works synergistically to break down mucus, reduce digestive discomfort, and promote overall gut health. Regular consumption can help alleviate symptoms of mucus in the stomach and promote a healthy digestive system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with mucus in stomach because they contain active compounds like gingerols and shogaols that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and ease digestion, making it easier to break down and eliminate excess mucus. Additionally, ginger has a natural expectorant effect, helping to thin and clear mucus from the stomach and digestive tract. This can provide relief from symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and indigestion associated with excess mucus in the stomach.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help reduce mucus in the stomach by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to mucus secretion and inflammation.
Shogaols: Shogaols possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, including reduced mucus production and inflammation in the stomach.

Volatile oils (specifically, gingerol-derived sesquiterpenes): These sesquiterpenes exhibit anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help reduce mucus secretion in the stomach by decreasing inflammation and promoting digestion.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stomach inflammation and mucus production.
Leaves: Although less commonly used, some herbal supplements use Zingiber officinale leaves due to their ability to aid digestion and reduce nausea, which can be beneficial in reducing stomach mucus.

Roots: Ginger roots, while not as commonly used as rhyzomes, are sometimes used in herbal supplements to help alleviate digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach, potentially reducing mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning when the dew is still present.
Clean the rhizomes by gently scrubbing them with a soft brush to remove any dirt or debris.
Slice the cleaned rhizomes into thin pieces about 1/8 inch thick using a sharp knife.
Dry the sliced rhizomes in a single layer on a paper bag or food dehydrator at 150-200°f for 24 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400-500 mg of dried zingiber officinale powder and seal the capsules securely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with mucus in stomach because they contain licorice root extract, a natural anti-inflammatory compound. The flavonoids and saponins present in the extract help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. By inhibiting the production of stomach acid and mucus, Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules alleviate symptoms of heartburn, bloating, and stomach discomfort associated with excessive mucus production, providing relief and promoting a healthy digestive system. Regular use can also help to maintain gut health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help to break down and reduce mucus in the stomach by inhibiting the production of mucus and reducing its viscosity.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation that can contribute to excess mucus production.

Trigonelline: This alkaloid has a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effect, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, thereby reducing mucus production and promoting a healthy gut environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are primarily used as they contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps in reducing inflammation and soothing stomach ulcers.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used, as they contain glycosides that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing stomach inflammation and soothing mucus.

Stems: The stems of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used, as they contain a mixture of flavonoids and glycosides that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help in reducing stomach inflammation and soothing mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier at a local health food store.
Clean the dried roots by washing them with filtered water to remove impurities.
Grind the cleaned roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 500g of gelatin in a bowl and add a few drops of water to bind.
Encapsulate the mixture in empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with mucus in stomach because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin present in these capsules has the ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. It also has mucolytic properties, which help break down excess mucus and improve gut motility. This results in the reduction of stomach mucus and alleviation of symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and indigestion, making it an effective natural remedy for digestive issues. Its anti-inflammatory effects also soothe the stomach lining.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and mucus production in the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC exhibits anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties, breaking down mucus in the stomach and facilitating its expulsion, thus providing relief from conditions like gastritis and dyspepsia.

Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMD has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce mucus production and prevent bacterial overgrowth in the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain turmeric, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce mucus production in the stomach.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain curcumin, a bioactive compound that can help alleviate symptoms of stomach ulcers and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Seeds are not as common but can be used to extract oil with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of curcuma longa rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 150g of the powder with 300g of gelatin in a bowl for 10 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the gelatin-powder mixture and produce 1000 capsules in 30 minutes.
Store the herbal curcuma longa capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum capsules helps with mucus in stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to stimulate digestion. The active compounds in Cinnamomum verum, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to reduce inflammation and mucus production in the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of indigestion and bloating. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum capsules have been traditionally used to aid in digestion and relieve nausea, making it a popular natural remedy for stomach issues. Its soothing effects can also help to reduce stomach acidity.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This constituent helps to reduce mucus in the stomach by inhibiting the production of gastric mucus and its protective action on the gastric mucosa.

Cinnamic acid: This constituent exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and mucus production in the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of stomach ulcers and gastritis.
Eugenol: This constituent has antibacterial properties that help to reduce the bacterial load in the stomach, which can contribute to the production of mucus and gastric ulcers.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cinnamon leaves are used to make capsules due to their carminative properties, which help in relieving stomach discomfort and reducing mucus production.
Barks: Cinnamon barks are used to make capsules due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing inflammation and congestion in the stomach.
Rhyzomes: Cinnamon rhyzomes are used to make capsules due to their anti-spasmodic properties, which help in relieving stomach cramps and reducing mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cinnamomum verum bark from a reputable supplier to ensure quality control.
Grind 20g of the bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 10g of tapioca starch and 5g of gelatin in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the mixture and seal them immediately to prevent contamination.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with mucus in stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The active compounds in Foeniculum vulgare, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort. By soothing the digestive tract and reducing mucus production, Foeniculum vulgare capsules can help to alleviate symptoms of stomach mucus, such as bloating, nausea, and heartburn. This natural remedy promotes a healthy digestive system and relieves digestive issues.

Foeniculin: It has carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and alleviate symptoms of bloating and discomfort associated with excess mucus in the stomach.
Bisabolol: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation caused by excess mucus, thereby relieving symptoms of stomach discomfort and nausea.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used to make capsules for digestive issues due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare are used to create capsules that help in reducing inflammation and relieving digestive issues.
Roots: The roots of the plant are also used to make capsules that aid in digestion and help alleviate symptoms of stomach mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 foeniculum vulgare stems with seeds when the seeds are fully mature and dry.
Dry the harvested stems in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare stems into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250mg of the powder with 250mg of a natural filler such as gum arabic in a ratio.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum capsules helps with mucus in stomach because they contain piperine, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. It works by inhibiting the production of mucus in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The piperine in these capsules also stimulates digestion and improves gut motility, helping to clear out excess mucus and promoting a healthy gut environment. This leads to relief from symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and indigestion. Regular use can help maintain a balanced stomach and prevent future occurrences of mucus buildup.

Terpene: Terpene has carminative properties, which help to reduce gas formation and alleviate stomach discomfort caused by mucus accumulation.
Flavonoid: Flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excessive mucus production.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Piper nigrum seeds are used to make capsules for mucus in the stomach because they contain piperine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues.
Fruits: Piper nigrum fruits are used to make capsules for mucus in the stomach because they are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues.

Leaves: Piper nigrum leaves are used to make capsules for mucus in the stomach because they contain volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried piper nigrum, 500 grams of gelatin capsules, and 25 grams of vitamin e oil.
Mix 250 grams of dried piper nigrum with 25 grams of vitamin e oil in a glass bowl.
Allow the mixture to sit for 30 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse and blend together.
Fill 500 gelatin capsules with the mixture using a spoon and a clean work surface.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trigonella foenum-graecum capsules helps with mucus in stomach because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compound, diosgenin, in these capsules works to thin and clear excess mucus from the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of indigestion and nausea. By reducing inflammation and promoting digestion, Trigonella foenum-graecum capsules also help to prevent the formation of mucus in the stomach, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall gut health. remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach, thereby alleviating excess mucus production.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Trigonella foenum-graecum capsules exhibit anti-secretory and anti-ulcerative properties, which help decrease stomach acid production and prevent the formation of excessive mucus.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in these capsules possess antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and the production of excess mucus.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and relieve digestive issues.
Leaves: They are used for their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the stomach and digestive tract.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried trigonella foenum-graecum flowers using a digital scale for accuracy.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a food processor for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered trigonella foenum-graecum with 4 grams of gelatin using a spoon.
Fill a 00 capsule with the mixture and seal it using a capsule filling machine.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dark place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus capsules helps with mucus in stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The herb's immune-boosting effects also help to clear excess mucus and promote a healthy balance of gut flora. This can aid in relieving symptoms of stomach mucus, such as bloating and nausea. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus has been traditionally used to enhance digestion and relieve stomach discomfort, making it a potential natural remedy for managing mucus in the stomach.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help reduce inflammation and mucus production in the stomach by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Flavonoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing mucus production and alleviating symptoms of digestive issues.
Polysaccharides: They exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to stomach mucus and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in active compounds, making them effective in treating digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: They contain bioactive molecules that help in reducing inflammation and soothing stomach ulcers.
Seeds: They possess antioxidant properties that aid in reducing mucus production in the stomach and promoting digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried astragalus membranaceus root from a trusted supplier or cultivate it yourself with proper care.
Dry the root further in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to reduce moisture.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with gelatin or vegetarian capsule filler in a ratio of 1:5 to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the blend using a capsule filling machine at a rate of 500 capsules per hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia angustifolia capsules helps with mucus in stomach because they contain a natural compound called sennoside. These sennosides stimulate bowel movements and enhance digestion, reducing the presence of mucus in the stomach. The anti-inflammatory properties of Cassia angustifolia help to soothe the digestive tract, calming inflammation and irritation that can contribute to excess mucus production. As a result, the capsules promote a healthy gut and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as bloating and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help break down mucus in the stomach by reducing the viscosity and thickness of the mucus, making it easier to expel.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can contribute to mucus production, and also help inhibit the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate mucus buildup.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach, break down mucus, and prevent the formation of new mucus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Cassia angustifolia are used to make capsules for mucus in the stomach because they contain sennosides, which are anthraquinone glycosides responsible for their purgative properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Cassia angustifolia are used to make capsules for mucus in the stomach because they contain a higher concentration of sennosides compared to other parts, making them more potent.
Leaves: The leaves of Cassia angustifolia are used to make capsules for mucus in the stomach because they also contain sennosides, although in lower concentrations than the roots and seeds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried cassia angustifolia roots and leaves by cutting them from mature plants.
Dry the harvested cassia angustifolia further at 50°c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 300g of dried cassia angustifolia into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix 20% of the cassia angustifolia powder with 80% gelatin powder to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the cassia angustifolia gelatin mixture using an automated capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum capsules helps with mucus in stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compounds in this herb, such as ferulic acid and essential oils, help to break down and clear out excess mucus in the stomach. By reducing inflammation and promoting mucus drainage, Zanthoxylum bungeanum capsules can provide relief from symptoms like bloating, nausea, and indigestion associated with stomach mucus. Regular use may help to improve digestion and reduce the risk of stomach problems.
remedy_constituents: Limonoids: They help reduce mucus in the stomach by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, thereby alleviating symptoms of stomach discomfort.
Phenolic acids (e.g. ferulic acid): They help dissolve and reduce mucus in the stomach by inhibiting the production of mucus and exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties.
Coumarins (e.g. psoralen): They help reduce mucus in the stomach by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate stomach discomfort and promote digestion.
remedy_parts: Barks: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their antispasmodic and expectorant properties that help in reducing stomach mucus.
Seeds: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that help in soothing the stomach and reducing mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves and branches in early morning when the plant is at its highest oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves and branches in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves and branches into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the zanthoxylum bungeanum powder with 2 parts of gelatin or vegetable cellulose and add a small amount of water.
Fill a gelatin capsule with 250mg of the zanthoxylum bungeanum mixture and seal it using a capsule machine.
